workflow_name: Internal Account Review Transcript -> Team Actions
user_message: Can you extract the security posture from this email thread for Contoso Ltd? We had a deep-dive last month -- need the follow-up.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: security posture Discussion - Contoso Ltd
Date: 2024-03-10
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Angela Doyle (Contoso Ltd), Marcus Williams (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Angela Doyle (Contoso Ltd): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on security posture from our last session.

[00:07:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): The timeline for stakeholder alignment is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:12:00] Angela Doyle (Contoso Ltd): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about integration complexity. Can we address those?

[00:15:00] Angela Doyle (Contoso Ltd): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about resource allocation. Can we address those?

[00:18:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on technical validation and found that there's a compliance requirement we hadn't accounted for in the original scope.

[00:20:00] Angela Doyle (Contoso Ltd): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is performance requirements.

handoffs.condition: team coaching or risk review input

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF52-I trap (Misattributes view to wrong team member); produces complete Team action items with owners, coaching notes (constructive frame), customer-facing draft (sanitized
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF52-I failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF52-I failure mode (Misattributes view to wrong team member)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct security posture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF52-I -- Misattributes view to wrong team member
